rationale: Assessment and strength session already completed today — full rest to absorb that stimulus.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.notes: Working weight 72.5 kg; full depth, flat heels

exercises.name: Barbell Conventional Deadlift
exercises.notes: Working weight 85 kg; brace hard, hip drive

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: 55 kg; elbows 45–60° from torso, no wide flare

exercises.name: Face Pulls (cable or band)
exercises.notes: Shoulder prehab — mandatory every upper session

exercises.name: Dumbbell Row
exercises.notes: Brace on bench; full ROM, controlled tempo

exercises.name: Forearm Plank
exercises.notes: Neutral spine; stop if hips sag

rationale: First structured session at baseline working weights — squat and deadlift both in the same session at RPE 7, well within the submaximal threshold, building the linear progression foundation.

exercises.name: Continuous Freestyle Swim
exercises.notes: Target avg HR 112–131 bpm; aim to match or beat 550 m

rationale: Zone 2 aerobic base session the day after heavy lifting — pool is low-impact and avoids interference with leg recovery.
Mid-week recovery day to separate the two strength sessions and allow adaptation.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.notes: Add 2.5 kg if Day 1 felt solid — try 75 kg

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Start ~60 kg; hinge pattern, slight knee bend

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Add 2.5 kg if Day 1 felt solid — try 57.5 kg

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Neutral or supinated grip; no behind-the-neck

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: Slow and controlled; lower back glued to floor

rationale: Second full-body strength session of the week — RDL replaces conventional deadlift to avoid heavy spinal load twice in the week while still training the posterior chain.
Rest day before the optional fourth session to ensure quality if taken.

exercises.name: Continuous Freestyle Swim
exercises.notes: Avg HR 112–131 bpm; note distance to track aerobic progress

rationale: Second weekly Zone 2 swim builds aerobic base toward VO2 max and run goals without adding joint stress or interfering with strength recovery.
